description:
You are an observability engineer validating how quickly a Prometheus-style
    textfile-exporter can be scraped.

    The metrics that would normally be served over HTTP have been written to the
    local filesystem so you can test the numbers without starting a web
    server.  Your goal is to use a single curl command to measure two things:

      • the exact number of bytes that would be downloaded  
      • the total time curl needed to read the file

    1. **Target “URL”**  
       file:///home/user/observability/metrics/exporter_stats.txt

    2. **Where to save the measurement**  
       /home/user/observability/curl_metrics.log

    3. **Required output format inside curl_metrics.log**  
       The file must contain *exactly* two newline-terminated lines, in this
       order (case-sensitive key names, no extra spaces):

           SIZE_DOWNLOAD=<number_of_bytes>
           TIME_TOTAL=<seconds_float>

       Example of the exact layout (the numbers are just examples – the first
       one must be correct, the second will naturally vary):

           SIZE_DOWNLOAD=76
           TIME_TOTAL=0.001234

       • There must be **no** additional whitespace before/after the “=” sign.  
       • There must be **no** blank lines before, between or after the two
         lines.  
       • The first line’s value **must match the exact byte size of the
         exporter_stats.txt file**.  
       • The second line may be any valid floating-point representation output
         by curl’s %{time_total} write-out token.

    4. **Hints (not mandatory, but useful)**  
       • You can discard the actual body with -o /dev/null.  
       • See “man curl” for the -w/--write-out option and its
         “size_download” and “time_total” tokens.

    The automated test will confirm:

       • /home/user/observability/curl_metrics.log exists.  
       • Line 1 equals “SIZE_DOWNLOAD=76”.  
       • Line 2 starts with “TIME_TOTAL=” and is followed by a valid number.

    Only the contents of curl_metrics.log are graded; nothing you print to
    stdout matters.  Perform the task entirely from the terminal.
truth:
<!-- Existing directory & data the agent will read --------------------->
    Directory: /home/user/observability/metrics
    File:      /home/user/observability/metrics/exporter_stats.txt

    Exact byte-for-byte contents of exporter_stats.txt (with LF line endings):

        up 1\n
        scrape_duration_seconds 0.005\n
        scrape_samples_post_metric_relabeling 15\n

    Concatenated into a single string (each “\n” is one 0x0A byte):

        "up 1\nscrape_duration_seconds 0.005\nscrape_samples_post_metric_relabeling 15\n"

    This file is therefore **76 bytes** long – the value the test expects on
    the first line of curl_metrics.log.

    -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    The test script will:

      1. Verify that /home/user/observability/curl_metrics.log exists.
      2. Read its first line and expect exactly:
            SIZE_DOWNLOAD=76
      3. Read its second line and verify it matches the regex:
            ^TIME_TOTAL=[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
         (any positive float allowed).
      4. Fail if any extra lines or whitespace are present.
name: task_000000_3b92109f
